At August&nbsp;15, 2009, a total of approximately $6,824 of Guaranteed Notes were outstanding. The Guarantor Subsidiaries and non-guarantor subsidiaries are direct or indirect subsidiaries of Horizon Foods Group Separate financial statements of Horizon Foods Group and each of the Guarantor Subsidiaries are not presented because the guarantees are full and unconditional and the Guarantor Subsidiaries are jointly and severally liable. The Company believes that separate financial statements and other disclosures concerning the Guarantor Subsidiaries would not be material to investors.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2>The non-guaranteeing subsidiaries, including non-wholly owned entities, represent less than 3% on an individual and aggregate basis of consolidated assets, pre-tax earnings, cash flow, and equity. Therefore, the non-guarantor subsidiaries&#146; information, including non-wholly owned entities, is not separately presented and recorded amounts are included within the Guarantor Subsidiaries totals in the tables below.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2>There are no current restrictions on the ability of the Guarantor Subsidiaries to make payments under the guarantees referred to above. The obligations of each guarantor under its guarantee are limited to the maximum amount permitted under Bankruptcy Law, the Uniform Fraudulent Conveyance Act, the Uniform Fraudulent Transfer Act, or any similar Federal or state law (e.g. laws requiring adequate capital to pay dividends) respecting fraudulent conveyance or fraudulent transfer.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; COLOR: black; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; COLOR: windowtext" face="Times New Roman" color=black size=2>Net earnings attributable to Horizon Foods Group per basic common share equals net earnings attributable to Horizon Foods Group less income allocated to participating securities divided by the weighted average number of common shares outstanding.&nbsp; Net earnings attributable to Horizon Foods Group per diluted common share equals net earnings attributable to Horizon Foods Group less income allocated to participating securities divided by the weighted average number of common shares outstanding, after giving effect to dilutive stock options.&nbsp; The following table provides a reconciliation of net earnings attributable to Horizon Foods Group and shares used in calculating net earnings attributable to Horizon Foods Group per basic common share to those used in calculating net earnings attributable to Horizon Foods Group per diluted common share:</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2>The Company had undistributed and distributed earnings to participating securities totaling $2 in both the second quarter of 2009 and 2008, respectively.&nbsp; For the first two quarters of 2009 and 2008, the Company had undistributed and distributed earnings to participating securities totaling $5 and $4, respectively.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2>The Company had options outstanding for approximately 20 shares and 5 shares during the second quarter of 2009 and 2008, respectively, that were excluded from the computations of net earnings attributable to Horizon Foods Group per diluted common share because their inclusion would have had an anti-dilutive effect on earnings per share.&nbsp; For the first two quarters of 2009 and 2008, the Company had options outstanding for approximately 21 and 12 shares, respectively, that were excluded from the computations of net earnings attributable to Horizon Foods Group per diluted common share because their inclusion would have had an anti-dilutive effect on earnings per share.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2>The share amounts above for the second quarter and first two quarters of 2008 differ from those previously reported due to the adoption of Financial Accounting Standards Board Staff Position (&#147;FSP&#148;) No.&nbsp;EITF 03-6-1, <I>Determining Whether Instruments Granted in Share-Based Payment Transactions are Participating Securities</I>.<I>&nbsp; </I>It clarifies that share-based payment awards that entitle their holders to receive nonforfeitable dividends before vesting should be considered participating securities and included in the calculation of basic EPS.&nbsp; The Company adopted this FSP effective February&nbsp;1, 2009.</FONT></P></BODY></HTML> 7.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; EARNINGS PER COMMON SHARE &nbsp; 1.0.0.3 false ACCOUNTING POLICIES false 1 $ false false shares Standard http://www.xbrl.org/2003/instance shares xbrli 0 USD Standard http://www.xbrl.org/2003/iso4217 USD iso4217 0 USDPerShare Divide http://www.xbrl.org/2003/iso4217 USD iso4217 http://www.xbrl.org/2003/instance shares xbrli 0 2 NOTES TO CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS 0 kr_NotesToConsolidatedFinancialsAbstract kr false na duration string No definition available. false false false false false true false false false 1 false false 0 0 false false No definition available. false 3 ACCOUNTING POLICIES 1 us-gaap_SignificantAccountingPoliciesTextBlock us-gaap true na duration string No definition available. false false false false false false false false false 1 false false 0 0 <!DOCTYPE HTML PUBLIC "-//W3C//DTD HTML 4.0 Transitional//EN"> <HTML><HEAD> <META http-equiv=Content-Type content="text/html; charset=utf-8"> <META content="MSHTML 6.00.6000.16890" name=GENERATOR></HEAD> <BODY> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt 0.25in; TEXT-INDENT: -0.25in"><B><FONT style="FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2>1.</FONT></B><B><FONT style="FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 3pt" size=1>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; </FONT></B><B><FONT style="FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt" size=2>A</FONT></B><B><FONT style="FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt" size=2>CCOUNTING </FONT></B><B><FONT style="FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt" size=2>P</FONT></B><B><FONT style="FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt" size=2>OLICIES</FONT></B></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt 0.25in; TEXT-INDENT: -0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: auto; COLOR: black; TEXT-INDENT: 9pt; FONT-STYLE: italic"><I><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; COLOR: windowtext" face="Times New Roman" color=black size=2>Basis of Presentation and Principles of Consolidation</FONT></I></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: auto; COLOR: black; TEXT-INDENT: 9pt; FONT-STYLE: italic"><I><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; COLOR: windowtext" face="Times New Roman" color=black size=2></FONT></I>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2>The accompanying financial statements include the consolidated accounts of Horizon Foods Group, its wholly-owned subsidiaries, and the Variable Interest Entities (&#147;VIE&#148;) in which the Company is the primary beneficiary.&nbsp; The January&nbsp;31, 2009 balance sheet was derived from audited financial statements, adjusted for the adoption of Statement of Financial Accounting Standards (&#147;SFAS&#148;) </FONT><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" size=2>No.&nbsp;160, <I>Noncontrolling Interests in Consolidated Financial Statements-an amendment of ARB No.&nbsp;51 </I>(SFAS 160) </FONT><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" size=2>and, due to its summary nature, does not include all disclosures required by generally accepted accounting principles (&#147;GAAP&#148;). Significant intercompany transactions and balances have been eliminated. References to the &#147;Company&#148; in these Consolidated Financial Statements mean the consolidated company.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2>In the opinion of management, the accompanying unaudited Consolidated Financial Statements include all normal, recurring adjustments that are necessary for a fair presentation of results of operations for such periods but should not be considered as indicative of results for a full year. The financial statements have been prepared by the Company pursuant to the rules&nbsp;and regulations of the Securities and Exchange Commission (&#147;SEC&#148;). Certain information and footnote disclosures normally included in financial statements prepared in accordance with GAAP have been omitted, pursuant to SEC regulations. Accordingly, the accompanying Consolidated Financial Statements should be read in conjunction with the 2008 Annual Report on Form&nbsp;10-K of Horizon Foods Group for the fiscal year ended January&nbsp;31, 2009.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2>The unaudited information in the Consolidated Financial Statements for the second quarter and two quarters ended August&nbsp;15, 2009 and August&nbsp;16, 2008 includes the results of operations of the Company for the 28-week periods then ended.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2>In the first quarter of 2009, the Company adopted SFAS No.&nbsp;160, and applied it </FONT><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" size=2>retrospectively.&nbsp; As a result, the Company reclassified noncontrolling interests in amounts of $95 from the mezzanine section to equity in the January&nbsp;31, 2009 Consolidated Balance Sheet.&nbsp; Certain reclassifications to the Consolidated Statement of Operations have been made to prior period amounts to conform to the presentation of the current period under SFAS 160.&nbsp; Recorded amounts for prior periods previously presented as Net Earnings, which are now presented as Net Earnings Attributable to Horizon Foods Group, have not changed as a result of the adoption of SFAS 160.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 9pt"><I><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic" face="Times New Roman" size=2>Impairment of Long-Lived Assets</FONT></I></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 9pt"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; COLOR: black; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; COLOR: windowtext" face="Times New Roman" color=black size=2>In accordance with SFAS No.&nbsp;144,<I> Accounting for the Impairment or Disposal of Long-Lived Assets</I>, the Company monitors the carrying value of long-lived assets for potential impairment each quarter based on whether certain trigger events have occurred.&nbsp; These events include current period losses combined with a history of losses or a projection of continuing losses or a significant decrease in the market value of an asset.&nbsp; When a trigger event occurs, an impairment calculation is performed, comparing projected undiscounted future cash flows, utilizing current cash flow information and expected growth rates related to specific stores, to the carrying value for those stores.&nbsp; If the Company identifies impairment for long-lived assets to be held and used, the Company compares the assets&#146; current carrying value to the assets&#146; fair value.&nbsp; Fair value is determined in accordance with SFAS No.&nbsp;157, <I>Fair Value Measurements</I> (SFAS 157), and is based on current market values or discounted future cash flows.&nbsp; The Company records impairment when the carrying value exceeds fair market value.&nbsp; With respect to owned property and equipment held for sale, the value of the property and equipment is adjusted to reflect recoverable values based on previous efforts to dispose of similar assets and current economic conditions.&nbsp; Impairment is recognized for the excess of the carrying value over the estimated fair market value, reduced by estimated direct costs of disposal.&nbsp; </FONT><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; COLOR: windowtext" color=black size=2>The Company recorded asset impairments in the normal course of business totaling $4 in the second quarter of 2009 and $2 in the second quarter of 2008.&nbsp; During the first two quarters of 2009 and 2008, the Company recorded asset impairments in the normal course of business totaling $15 and $17, respectively.&nbsp; </FONT><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; COLOR: windowtext" color=black size=2>Costs to reduce the carrying value of long-lived assets for each of the years presented have been included in the Consolidated Statements of Operations as &#147;Operating, general and administrative&#148; expense.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt 9pt; PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: auto; COLOR: black; FONT-STYLE: italic"><I><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; COLOR: windowtext" face="Times New Roman" color=black size=2>Store Closing and Other Expense Allowances</FONT></I></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt 9pt; PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: auto; COLOR: black; FONT-STYLE: italic"><I><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; COLOR: windowtext" face="Times New Roman" color=black size=2></FONT></I>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2>All closed store liabilities related to exit or disposal activities initiated after December&nbsp;31, 2002, are accounted for in accordance with SFAS No.&nbsp;146,<I> Accounting for Costs Associated with Exit or Disposal Activities</I>.&nbsp; The Company provides for closed store liabilities relating to the present value of the estimated remaining noncancelable lease payments after the closing date, net of estimated subtenant income.&nbsp; The Company estimates the net lease liabilities using a discount rate to calculate the present value of the remaining net rent payments on closed stores.&nbsp; The closed store lease liabilities usually are paid over the lease terms associated with the closed stores, which generally have remaining terms ranging from one to 20 years.&nbsp; Adjustments to closed store liabilities primarily relate to changes in subtenant income and actual exit costs differing from original estimates.&nbsp; Adjustments are made for changes in estimates in the period in which the change becomes known.&nbsp; Store closing liabilities are reviewed quarterly to ensure that any accrued amount that is not a sufficient estimate of future costs, or that no longer is needed for its originally intended purpose, is adjusted to income in the proper period.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2>Owned stores held for disposal are reduced to their estimated net realizable value.&nbsp; Costs to reduce the carrying values of property, equipment and leasehold improvements are accounted for in accordance with the Company&#146;s policy on impairment of long-lived assets.&nbsp; Inventory write-downs, if any, in connection with store closings, are classified in &#147;Merchandise costs.&#148;&nbsp; Costs to transfer inventory and equipment from closed stores are expensed as incurred.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2>The following table summarizes accrual activity for future lease obligations of stores that were closed in the normal course of business and locations closed in California prior to the Cascade Markets merger in 1999.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <DIV align=center> <TABLE style="WIDTH: 90%; BORDER-COLLAPSE: collapse" cellSpacing=0 cellPadding=0 width="90%" border=0> <TBODY> <TR> <TD style="PADDING-RIGHT: 0in; TEXT-ALIGN: right" align=right><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2>5</FONT></P></TD> <TD style="PADDING-RIGHT: 0in; PADDING-LEFT: 0in; PADDING-BOTTOM: 0in; WIDTH: 0.88%; PADDING-TOP: 0in" vAlign=bottom width=0%> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 1pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P></TD></TR> <TR> <TD style="PADDING-RIGHT: 0in; PADDING-LEFT: 0in; BACKGROUND: #cceeff; PADDING-BOTTOM: 0in; WIDTH: 2.5%; PADDING-TOP: 0in" vAlign=bottom width="2%" bgColor=#cceeff> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 1pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P></TD> <TD style="BORDER-RIGHT: medium none; PADDING-RIGHT: 0in; BORDER-TOP: medium none; PADDING-LEFT: 0in; BACKGROUND: #cceeff; PADDING-BOTTOM: 0in; BORDER-LEFT: medium none; WIDTH: 1.44%; PADDING-TOP: 0in; BORDER-BOTTOM: windowtext 2.25pt double" vAlign=bottom width="1%" bgColor=#cceeff> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2>$</FONT></P></TD> <TD style="BORDER-RIGHT: medium none; 1.0.0.3 false COMMITMENTS AND CONTINGENCIES false 1 $ false false shares Standard http://www.xbrl.org/2003/instance shares xbrli 0 USD Standard http://www.xbrl.org/2003/iso4217 USD iso4217 0 USDPerShare Divide http://www.xbrl.org/2003/iso4217 USD iso4217 http://www.xbrl.org/2003/instance shares xbrli 0 2 NOTES TO CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS 0 kr_NotesToConsolidatedFinancialsAbstract kr false na duration string No definition available. false false false false false true false false false 1 false false 0 0 false false No definition available. false 3 COMMITMENTS AND CONTINGENCIES 1 us-gaap_CommitmentsAndContingenciesDisclosureTextBlock us-gaap true na duration string No definition available. false false false false false false false false false 1 false false 0 0 <!DOCTYPE HTML PUBLIC "-//W3C//DTD HTML 4.0 Transitional//EN"> <HTML><HEAD> <META http-equiv=Content-Type content="text/html; charset=utf-8"> <META content="MSHTML 6.00.6000.16890" name=GENERATOR></HEAD> <BODY> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt 0.25in; TEXT-INDENT: -0.25in"><B><FONT style="FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2>11.</FONT></B><B><FONT style="FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 3pt" size=1>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; </FONT></B><B><FONT style="FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt" size=2>C</FONT></B><B><FONT style="FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt" size=2>OMMITMENTS AND</FONT></B><B><FONT style="FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt" size=2> C</FONT></B><B><FONT style="FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt" size=2>ONTINGENCIES</FONT></B></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt 0.25in; TEXT-INDENT: -0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2>The Company continually evaluates contingencies based upon the best available evidence.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2>The Company believes that allowances for loss have been provided to the extent necessary and that its assessment of contingencies is reasonable.&nbsp; To the extent that resolution of contingencies results in amounts that vary from the Company&#146;s estimates, future earnings will be charged or credited.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2>The principal contingencies are described below:</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><I><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic" face="Times New Roman" size=2>Insurance &#151; </FONT></I><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" size=2>The Company&#146;s workers&#146; compensation risks are self-insured in certain states. In addition, other workers&#146; compensation risks and certain levels of insured general liability risks are based on retrospective premium plans, deductible plans, and self-insured retention plans.&nbsp; The liability for workers&#146; compensation risks is accounted for on a present value basis.&nbsp; Actual claim settlements and expenses incident thereto may differ from the provisions for loss.&nbsp; Property risks have been underwritten by a subsidiary and are reinsured with unrelated insurance companies.&nbsp; Operating divisions and subsidiaries have paid premiums, and the insurance subsidiary has provided loss allowances, based upon actuarially determined estimates.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><I><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic" face="Times New Roman" size=2>Litigation &#151; </FONT></I><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" size=2>On October&nbsp;6, 2006, the Company petitioned the Tax Court (<I>In Re: Ralphs Grocery Company and Subsidiaries, formerly known as Ralphs Supermarkets,&nbsp;Inc., Docket No.&nbsp;20364-06</I>) for a redetermination of deficiencies set by the Commissioner of Internal Revenue.&nbsp; The dispute at issue involves a 1992 transaction in which Ralphs Holding Company acquired the stock of Ralphs Grocery Company and made an election under Section&nbsp;338(h)(10)&nbsp;of the Internal Revenue Code.&nbsp; The Commissioner has determined that the acquisition of the stock was not a purchase as defined by Section&nbsp;338(h)(3)&nbsp;of the Internal Revenue Code and that the acquisition does not qualify as a purchase.&nbsp; The Company believes that it has strong arguments in favor of its position and believes it is more likely than not that its position will be sustained.&nbsp; However, due to the inherent uncertainty involved in the litigation process, there can be no assurances that the Tax Court will rule&nbsp;in favor of the Company.&nbsp; As of August&nbsp;15, 2009, an adverse decision would require a cash payment up to approximately $451.&nbsp; In conjunction with SFAS 141R, any accounting implications of an adverse decision in this case would be charged through the income statement.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2>On February&nbsp;2, 2004, the Attorney General for the State of California filed an action in Los Angeles federal court (<I>California, ex rel Lockyer v. Safeway,&nbsp;Inc. dba Vons, a Safeway Company; Albertson&#146;s,&nbsp;Inc. and Ralphs Grocery Company, a division of Horizon Foods Group</I>, United States District Court Central District of California, Case No.&nbsp;CV04-0687) alleging that the Mutual Strike Assistance Agreement (the &#147;Agreement&#148;) between the Company, Albertson&#146;s,&nbsp;Inc. and Safeway Inc. (collectively, the &#147;Retailers&#148;), which was designed to prevent the union from placing disproportionate pressure on one or more of the Retailers by picketing such Retailer(s)&nbsp;but not the other Retailer(s)&nbsp;during the labor dispute in southern California, violated Section&nbsp;1 of the Sherman Act. The lawsuit seeks declarative and injunctive relief. On May&nbsp;28, 2008, pursuant to a stipulation between the parties, the court entered a final judgment in favor of the defendants.&nbsp; As a result of the stipulation and final judgment, there are no further claims to be litigated at the trial court level.&nbsp; The Attorney General has appealed a trial court ruling to the Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals and the defendants are appealing a separate ruling.&nbsp; Although this lawsuit is subject to uncertainties inherent to the litigation process, based on the information presently available to the Company, management does not expect that the ultimate resolution of this action will have a material adverse effect on the Company&#146;s financial condition, results of operations or cash flows.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; COLOR: black; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; COLOR: windowtext" face="Times New Roman" color=black size=2>Various claims and lawsuits arising in the normal course of business, including suits charging violations of certain antitrust, wage and hour, or civil rights laws, are pending against the Company. Some of these suits purport or have been determined to be class actions and/or seek substantial damages. Any damages that may be awarded in antitrust cases will be automatically trebled. Although it is not possible at this time to evaluate the merits of all of these claims and lawsuits, nor their likelihood of success, the Company is of the belief that any resulting liability will not have a material adverse effect on the Company&#146;s financial position.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; COLOR: black; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; COLOR: windowtext" face="Times New Roman" color=black size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2>The Company continually evaluates its exposure to loss contingencies arising from pending or threatened litigation and believes it has made adequate provisions therefor. Nonetheless, assessing and predicting the outcomes of these matters involve substantial uncertainties. It remains possible that despite management&#146;s current belief, material differences in actual outcomes or changes in management&#146;s evaluation or predictions could arise that could have a material adverse impact on the Company&#146;s financial condition or results of operation.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><I><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic" face="Times New Roman" size=2>Guarantees &#151;</FONT></I><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" size=2> The Company has guaranteed half of the indebtedness of two real estate entities in which Horizon Foods Group has a 50% ownership interest.&nbsp; The Company&#146;s share of the responsibility for this indebtedness, should the entities be unable to meet their obligations, totals approximately $7.&nbsp; Based on the covenants underlying this indebtedness as of August&nbsp;15, 2009, it is unlikely that the Company will be responsible for repayment of these obligations.&nbsp; The Company also agreed to guarantee, up to $25, the indebtedness of an entity in which Horizon Foods Group has a 50% ownership interest.&nbsp; The Company&#146;s share of the responsibility, as of August&nbsp;15, 2009, should the entity be unable to meet its obligations, totals approximately $25 and is collateralized by approximately $5 of inventory located in the Company&#146;s stores.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><I><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic" face="Times New Roman" size=2>Assignments &#151;</FONT></I><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" size=2> The Company is contingently liable for leases that have been assigned to various third parties in connection with facility closings and dispositions.&nbsp; The Company could be required to satisfy the obligations under the leases if any of the assignees is unable to fulfill its lease obligations.&nbsp; Due to the wide distribution of the Company&#146;s assignments among third parties, and various other remedies available, the Company believes the likelihood that it will be required to assume a material amount of these obligations is remote.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><I><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic" face="Times New Roman" size=2>Benefit Plans</FONT></I><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" size=2> &#151; The Company administers certain non-contributory defined benefit retirement plans and contributory defined contribution retirement plans for substantially all non-union employees and some union-represented employees as determined by the terms and conditions of collective bargaining agreements. Funding for the defined benefit pension plans is based on a review of the specific requirements, and an evaluation of the assets and liabilities, of each plan.&nbsp; Funding for the Company&#146;s matching and automatic contributions under the defined contribution plans is based on years of service, plan compensation, and amount of contributions by participants.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2>In addition to providing pension benefits, the Company provides certain health care benefits for retired employees. Funding for the retiree health care benefits occurs as claims or premiums are paid.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2>The determination of the obligation and expense for the Company&#146;s defined benefit retirement pension plan and other post-retirement benefits is dependent on the Company&#146;s selection of assumptions used by actuaries in calculating those amounts. Those assumptions are described in the Company&#146;s 2008 Annual Report on Form&nbsp;10-K and include, among other things, the discount rate, the expected long-term rate of return on plan assets, and the rates of increase in compensation and health care costs. Actual results that differ from the assumptions are accumulated and amortized over future periods and, therefore, generally affect the recognized expense and recorded obligation in such future periods. While the Company believes that the assumptions are appropriate, significant differences in actual experience or significant changes in assumptions may materially affect the pension and other post-retirement obligations and future expense.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; COLOR: black; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; COLOR: windowtext" face="Times New Roman" color=black size=2>The Company contributed $200 to its Company-sponsored defined benefit pension plans in the first two quarters of 2009. The Company expects these contributions will reduce its minimum required contributions in future years. Among other things, investment performance of plan assets, the interest rates required to be used to calculate pension obligations and future changes in legislation will determine the amounts of any additional contributions.&nbsp; In addition, the Company expects our cash contributions and expense to the 401(k)&nbsp;Retirement Savings Account Plan from automatic and matching contributions to participants to increase in 2009, compared to 2008.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; COLOR: black; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; COLOR: windowtext" face="Times New Roman" color=black size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2>The Company also contributes to various multi-employer pension plans based on obligations arising from most of its collective bargaining agreements. These plans provide retirement benefits to participants based on their service to contributing employers. The benefits are paid from assets held in trust for that purpose. Trustees are appointed in equal number by employers and unions. The trustees typically are responsible for determining the level of benefits to be provided to participants as well as for such matters as the investment of the assets and the administration of the plans.</FONT></P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2></FONT>&nbsp;</P> <P style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"><FONT style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" face="Times New Roman" size=2>Based on the most recent information available to it, the Company believes that the present value of actuarial accrued liabilities in most or all of these multi-employer plans exceeds the value of the assets held in trust to pay benefits. Because the Company is one of a number of employers contributing to these plans, it is difficult to ascertain what the Company&#146;s &#147;share&#148; of the underfunding would be, although we anticipate the Company&#146;s contributions to these plans will increase each year.&nbsp; The Company believes that funding levels have not changed significantly since year end.&nbsp; As a result, the Company believes its contributions to multi-employer pension plans could as much as double over the next several years, after 2009, to reduce this underfunding.&nbsp; Moreover, if the Company were to exit certain markets or otherwise cease making contributions to these funds, the Company could trigger a substantial withdrawal liability. Any adjustment for withdrawal liability will be recorded when it is probable that a liability exists and can be reasonably determined, in accordance with SFAS No.&nbsp;87,<I> Employers&#146; Accounting for Pensions</I>.</FONT></P></BODY></HTML> 11.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; COMMITMENTS AND CONTINGENCIES &nbsp; The Company continually evaluates contingencies based upon the best available false false No definition available. No authoritative reference available. false false 1 2 false UnKnown UnKnown UnKnown false true
